Wald scale-coefficient intervals calibrated — docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-07-09-nongaussian-unstructured-coverage-pilot (phase 2)

drm_validate_reml_spec aborts "REML is implemented only for univariate and bivariate Gaussian models" before any effect_type/provider dispatch. Blanket rejection: covers every mu effect_type (fixed/ordinary_re_intercept/ordinary_re_slope/structured) and every provider for nbinom2, not just this fixed cell -- no separate REML row is given per effect_type/provider below.
Same blanket REML gate as the mu REML row above; covers every sigma effect_type and provider for nbinom2.

Independent random intercept, e.g. "count ~ x + (1|id)". Single-seed test: |bias|<0.30 vs simulated truth, RE-vs-truth correlation r>0.35, sdr$pdHess=TRUE. Cannot combine with any sigma random effect, ordinary or structured (R/drmTMB.R:7543-7549, "cannot be combined").

Only the INDEPENDENT numeric slope form "(0+x|id)" is implemented (fit jointly with the intercept row's test); the correlated form "(1+x|id)" (term$type=="correlated_slope") is rejected by validate_poisson_mu_random_terms ("Only independent NB2 mu random intercepts and slopes are implemented").

Independent random intercept, e.g. "sigma ~ z + (1|id)". Single-seed test: |estimate-truth|<0.35, RE-vs-truth correlation r>0.30, sdr$pdHess=TRUE. Cannot combine with any mu random effect, ordinary or structured (R/drmTMB.R:7543-7549).

sigma ~ z + (0+z|id) and labelled "sigma ~ z + (1|p|id)" both error "Only independent NB2 sigma random intercepts are implemented in this slice"; sigma random slopes remain planned per the family docstring (R/family.R:344-347).

count ~ x + phylo(1|species, tree=tree), q1 location intercept. Rorqual SLURM job 14897050: 320/320 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false, bias -0.028, RMSE 0.096. Recovery-only; interval_status/coverage_status are unsupported/planned on the board.
count ~ x + spatial(1|site, coords=coords). Rorqual SLURM job 14897050: 320/320 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false, bias -0.042, RMSE 0.162. Recovery-only.
count ~ x + animal(1|id, Ainv=Q). Rorqual SLURM job 14918220: 320/320 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false, 35/320 near-zero estimates (0.109), bias -0.059, RMSE 0.165. Recovery-only.
count ~ x + relmat(1|id, Q=Q). Rorqual SLURM job 14918220: 320/320 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false, 36/320 near-zero estimates (0.1125), bias -0.045, RMSE 0.166. Recovery-only.

Pair-level Kronecker-precision q1 intercept, "count ~ x + phylo_interaction(1|plant:pollinator, tree1=, tree2=)". Combined Rorqual jobs 14918220+14936834: 160/160 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false, bias -0.055, RMSE 0.168. Recovery-only.
count ~ x + phylo(1+x|species, tree=tree), intercept plus one independent numeric slope. Rorqual array 14916938: 80/80 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false. Recovery-only.
count ~ x + spatial(1+x|site, coords=coords). Combined Rorqual array 14916938 + top-up job 14936279: 160/160 fit_ok, 2/160 pdHess false (top-up alone 0/80). Recovery-only.
count ~ x + animal(1+x|id, Ainv=Q). Rorqual array 14916938: 80/80 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false. Recovery-only.
count ~ x + relmat(1+x|id, Q=Q). Rorqual array 14916938: 80/80 fit_ok, 0 pdHess false. Recovery-only.

Slope-only structured mu terms, e.g. "spatial(0+x|site, coords=coords)", fail the generic count_supported_term gate ("intercept-only or one-slope"). Representative row: the identical rejection applies to phylo/animal/relmat/phylo_interaction slope-only forms -- NB2 never sets allow_slope_only_structured_mu=TRUE (contrast the Poisson-spatial carve-out at R/drmTMB.R:5301-5302).

Labelled structured mu, e.g. "phylo(1|p|species, tree=tree)" matched against a parallel labelled sigma term (the Gaussian-style mean-scale phylo/spatial/animal/relmat correlation route), is rejected: the NB2 validate_count_structured_mu_term call never sets allow_labelled_scalar_structured_mu=TRUE (contrast Poisson-spatial, R/drmTMB.R:5297-5298). No NB2 test exercises this path.

Combining a structured mu term with an ordinary mu bar term, e.g. "phylo(1|species,tree=tree) + (1|id)", errors "cannot be combined with ordinary mu random effects in this first gate". Representative row for all q1-intercept providers.

Exception to "one structured type per model": exactly two intercept-only providers drawn from {phylo,spatial,animal,relmat} (not phylo_interaction) may co-occur as primary+secondary q1 fields, e.g. "spatial(1|site,coords=coords) + relmat(1|id,Q=Q)", non-zi only (M5 row 105). Local crossed recovery ladder: n_lvl in {10,20,30}, 30 seeds/rung, 100/100 converged, pdHess=TRUE; a non-crossed control confirms separability. Recovery-only, no denominator/coverage.

nbinom2() has one response and no multi-endpoint covariance layout is reachable (q2/q4/q6/q8/q12): those layouts require paired mu1/mu2 endpoints or matched mu&sigma labelled terms, neither of which the NB2 formula gate admits (see the labelled-scalar rejection row above). Representative row standing for the whole q2-q12 region across every provider and both dpars.
mvbind() two-response shorthand is rejected outright: "mvbind shorthand is only available for two-response Gaussian models... NB2 models currently support one count response."

Intercept-only structured sigma, e.g. "sigma ~ phylo(1|species,tree=tree)", errors "currently support only unlabelled intercept-plus-one-slope structured terms". Representative row: identical rejection applies to spatial/animal/relmat intercept-only sigma terms; only the intercept-plus-one-slope form is even syntactically admitted (see the four rows below).

phylo_interaction()'s documented fitted slice is location-only ("wherever the existing q=1 structured mu machinery is active"); no sigma route is built, validated, or tested for nbinom2.

evidence_source: same basis as the mu q2-q12 representative row above
notes: No multi-endpoint sigma covariance layout exists for nbinom2 (single response; mu and sigma random effects are mutually exclusive per R/drmTMB.R:7543-7549 and 7378-7384). Representative row standing for q2-q12 across all sigma providers.
